title: ibuki Feature Change Request
description: Atomic unit of the ibuki proposal workflow. Submitted via MCP, validated against this schema by the rule engine before the LLM judge is invoked. Accepted proposals become locked feature specs; locked specs bundle into release manifests.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the payload conforms to. Semver string; must match the directory under schema/. Embedded in every payload so the rule engine can route to the correct validator.

tenant.description: GitHub organization slug. Defines the tenant boundary (one tenant per GitHub org).

project.description: GitHub repository name within the tenant org.

feature_id.description: Identifier for this feature, unique within (tenant, project). Kebab-case slug; convention is `feature-NNN-short-description` or a stable slug.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the feature.

intent.description: Why this feature exists. The outcome the proposer is trying to reach or the problem they are trying to solve.

scope.description: What is and is not covered by this proposal.

in.description: Bullet items explicitly in scope. At least one item required.

out.description: Bullet items explicitly out of scope. May be empty.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable conditions that define done. Prefer EARS notation (Ubiquitous, Event-driven, State-driven, Unwanted, Optional). At least one criterion required.

supersedes.description: Optional foreign-key reference to a feature_id this proposal replaces. On lock, the superseded feature transitions to state=superseded.

proposer.description: Human or agent that submitted this proposal.

type.description: Whether the proposer is a human or an automated agent.

id.description: Stable identifier for the proposer (e.g. GitHub username for humans, agent identifier for agents).

submitted_at.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp at which this payload was submitted. Must be UTC (suffix `Z`) or an explicit offset.

state.description: Lifecycle state of this feature proposal. New submissions start as `proposed`; the proposal workflow transitions to `locked`, `superseded`, or `retired`.
